title: Frontend integration: portfolio attribution/MCTR/benchmark, crash timeline, market valuation dashboard

observation.gap_identified: Backend returns rich analytics (attribution, MCTR, benchmark analytics, crash timeline, market valuation) via API endpoints, but frontend never renders them — users can't see 5+ major analytics that are already computed

implementation.feature_built: Wired 5 standalone backend analytics into user-facing frontend pages: (1) Brinson-Fachler performance attribution on portfolio page showing allocation/selection/interaction effects, (2) MCTR risk contributor visualization with bar chart on portfolio page, (3) Benchmark analytics panel (tracking error, IR, active share, capture ratios) on portfolio page, (4) 60-month crash probability timeline with ComposedChart (bar + line) and contributing risk factors on crash page, (5) Market valuation dashboard (CAPE, forward P/E, ERP, Buffett indicator, composite score) on outlook page

limitations: No new backend tests added (pure frontend integration)
Did not add frontend unit tests for new components
Portfolio attribution/MCTR depend on having 2+ holdings to show meaningful data
Crash timeline and valuation fetch on page load which may slow initial render
assessment.self_critique: Could have also integrated options intelligence, stock fundamentals (Piotroski), or pair analysis into frontend pages. Focused on the highest-impact integrations first. The portfolio page now has a lot of content — might benefit from collapsible sections in a future UI cycle.

next_steps: Add options intelligence panel to stock detail page (IV skew, P/C ratio, max pain visualization)
Add stock fundamentals (Piotroski F-Score) to stock detail page
Add pair analysis / spread trading dashboard as new page
Add collapsible/tab sections to portfolio page to manage content density
Consider lazy-loading crash timeline and valuation to avoid blocking initial render
